refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters, applies deterministic algorithms (physics-based cycle time estimation, energy models, availability calculations), and outputs a structured performance requirement set. Baseline and history managed in a versioned configuration database with audit logging.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Consensus – Performance requirements derived collaboratively across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, each contributing domain-specific estimates (e.g., Vehicle provides mechanical cycle time, Safety Services provides safety margin). Results merged and recorded centrally.

DP-C: Template-Based Requirement Mapping – Pre-defined requirement templates matched against worksite mission context via lookup tables and heuristics. Minimal computation; fast baseline generation. History tracked via configuration management system.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Real-Time Calibration – Baseline requirements derived offline using DP-A logic; continuously refined during operation using telemetry feedback (actual cycle times, energy consumption, availability metrics). Calibration updates trigger history entries.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with Versioned Configuration Database and Audit Logging

potential_sources: DP-A requires inputs from Vehicle (mechanical parameters, energy models) and Safety Services (safety constraint definitions). These are external dependencies, not coupling within DP-A itself.
DP-A outputs performance requirements that feed downstream design decisions (e.g., Vehicle powertrain sizing, Teleoperation bandwidth allocation). This is intentional hierarchical flow, not coupling.
Audit logging and versioning are self-contained within the derivation engine; no circular dependencies detected.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because it centralizes the derivation logic and database, reducing diffusion of requirement-generation responsibility. External dependencies (Vehicle parameters, Safety constraints) are well-defined inputs, not hidden couplings. Audit trail is intrinsic to the DP, not scattered across modules.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is provided in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema) by Site Manager or external planning system.
ASSUMPTION: Physics-based algorithms for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are well-validated and stable (not subject to frequent revision).

RISK: If worksite mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation engine may produce invalid or unsafe performance targets. Mitigation: Implement validation rules and fallback to conservative defaults.
RISK: Centralized engine becomes a bottleneck if derivation is computationally expensive or if updates are frequent. Mitigation: Pre-compute common scenarios; cache results.

RISK: High safety criticality demands that all derivation logic be formally verified or certified. Mitigation: Allocate verification effort; consider third-party validation for safety-critical algorithms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit specification of: (1) input schema for worksite mission context; (2) algorithm specifications for cycle time, energy, availability, and safety margin derivation; (3) baseline metadata structure; (4) audit log schema and retention policy. These should be detailed in the DP specification document.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A), the versioned configuration database, and audit logging infrastructure. It orchestrates inputs from Vehicle and Safety Services modules and exposes performance requirement baselines and history to Site Manager and System Engineer stakeholders via reporting interfaces.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) ingested from Site Manager planning system or external mission definition tool. Structured as mission task specification.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Performance requirement baselines, derived targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins), and baseline metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method) transmitted to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for operational planning and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraints and margin definitions received from Safety Services module (in); safety-critical performance targets and audit trail of safety-related requirement changes transmitted to Safety Services for compliance verification (out).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Baseline and history records synchronized across distributed NGHE Solution instances (if multi-site deployment); audit logs transmitted to central compliance/quality system for archival and reporting.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary and natural owner of cross-cutting performance derivation logic, baseline management, and audit infrastructure. Containment is high because the derivation engine, configuration database, and audit logging are system-wide concerns, not specific to Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Minimal diffusion: only external interface definitions (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module owns mechanical and powertrain subsystems but does not own the derivation engine or baseline management. If performance derivation were assigned to Vehicle, it would be tightly coupled to vehicle-specific parameters and inaccessible to Teleoperation or Safety Services. High diffusion: requirement derivation logic would be scattered across multiple modules if each contributed its own algorithms. Not recommended.

Teleoperation module owns remote control and communication subsystems. Performance derivation is not a teleoperation-specific concern. Assignment would create artificial coupling between remote operation and requirement management. High diffusion: logic would be isolated from Vehicle and Safety Services. Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety monitoring, constraint enforcement, and alert generation. While safety margins are part of performance derivation, the full derivation engine (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) is not a safety-specific concern. If assigned to Safety Services, non-safety performance targets would be orphaned or duplicated. Moderate diffusion. Not recommended as sole owner; Safety Services should provide safety constraint inputs to NGHE Solution's derivation engine.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) is a system-level cross-cutting concern that must integrate inputs from Vehicle (mechanical parameters), Safety Services (safety constraints), and worksite mission context (Tasking). Ownership by NGHE Solution ensures: (1) centralized, non-duplicated derivation logic; (2) unified baseline and history management; (3) clear traceability from mission inputs to derived requirements; (4) audit trail accessible to all stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality). Containment score of 95 reflects that the derivation engine, database, and audit infrastructure are intrinsic to system-level requirement management, not specific to any single subsystem.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module is not yet fully defined or is too coarse-grained, ownership may be unclear. Mitigation: Ensure NGHE Solution is explicitly scoped as the system integration layer responsible for cross-cutting concerns like requirement derivation and configuration management.

RISK: Vehicle and Safety Services modules may resist providing inputs (mechanical parameters, safety constraints) if they perceive loss of autonomy. Mitigation: Define clear, well-documented input/output contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert interfaces) to establish mutual dependencies and shared responsibility.

RISK: Audit logging and versioning infrastructure may be underestimated in scope or complexity. Mitigation: Allocate dedicated effort to design and implement configuration management and audit logging; consider leveraging existing enterprise tools (e.g., Git, database versioning, compliance frameworks).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) explicitly define the NGHE Solution module boundary and its responsibilities in the system architecture document; (2) specify the input/output contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces; (3) detail the configuration database schema and audit log structure; (4) allocate ownership of the derivation engine implementation to a specific team or subsystem within NGHE Solution (e.g., Performance Management subsystem).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) ingests worksite mission context via structured input; (2) computes performance requirements using deterministic algorithms; (3) records baselines with metadata; (4) maintains managed history with audit trail; (5) enables traceability reporting. DP-A is neither over-constrained nor under-specified. No coupling detected. Assumptions and risks are documented and mitigable.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): If NGHE Solution module is not yet fully scoped, ownership of the derivation engine may be ambiguous. Mitigation: Ensure NGHE Solution is explicitly defined as the system integration layer.

DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): Vehicle and Safety Services modules must provide inputs (mechanical parameters, safety constraints) to the derivation engine. If these inputs are not well-defined or are subject to frequent change, the derivation engine may become a bottleneck or source of rework. Mitigation: Establish stable, versioned input contracts.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment score of 95 for NGHE Solution reflects that the derivation engine, configuration database, and audit logging are system-level concerns. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimal. No evidence of diffusion across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules. IM assignment is sound.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible: (1) test harness can inject worksite mission context and verify derived performance targets; (2) baseline recording can be inspected via database queries; (3) history log can be audited for completeness and accuracy; (4) traceability chain can be validated via automated reporting. High safety criticality requires formal verification of derivation algorithms and audit logging, which is achievable with dedicated effort.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the derivation engine, database, and audit infrastructure. Vehicle and Safety Services provide inputs via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). Site Manager provides mission context via Tasking interface. System Engineer and Maintenance/Quality access baselines and history via reporting interfaces. No ambiguity in responsibility or interface ownership.

recommended_revisions: Ensure NGHE Solution module is explicitly scoped and documented as the system integration layer responsible for cross-cutting concerns.

Define detailed specifications for: (1) worksite mission context input schema; (2) performance requirement derivation algorithms; (3) baseline metadata structure; (4) audit log schema and retention policy.
Establish formal verification plan for derivation algorithms, given high safety criticality.
Define interface contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link with explicit data schemas and synchronization protocols.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been successfully refined, a sound DP selected, and a clear IM assignment made to NGHE Solution. All gates pass. However, the refined FR contains five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that are not yet atomic and require further decomposition. Specifically: (1) CR-NGHE-005.1 (ingest mission context) requires definition of input validation, schema enforcement, and error handling; (2) CR-NGHE-005.2 (compute requirements) requires detailed algorithm specifications for cycle time, energy, availability, and safety margin derivation; (3) CR-NGHE-005.3 (record baseline) requires database schema and metadata management; (4) CR-NGHE-005.4 (maintain history) requires audit logging, versioning, and retention policies; (5) CR-NGHE-005.5 (traceability reporting) requires report generation and query interfaces. Each sub-FR warrants a dedicated decomposition level to ensure completeness and traceability.
decision.justification_detail: The current level has established the system-level requirement and architectural ownership. The next level must decompose each sub-FR into atomic functional requirements, design parameters, and implementation modules. This is necessary to: (1) ensure all acceptance criteria are met (documented performance targets, traceable baselines, managed history); (2) allocate detailed design and implementation work to specific teams; (3) establish verification and validation criteria for each sub-function; (4) manage high safety criticality by ensuring all safety-critical algorithms and audit mechanisms are explicitly specified and verified.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and validate worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is provided in a structured, machine-readable format by Site Manager or external planning system.
Physics-based algorithms for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are well-validated and stable.
NGHE Solution module is explicitly scoped as the system integration layer responsible for cross-cutting concerns.
Vehicle and Safety Services modules will provide stable, versioned inputs (mechanical parameters, safety constraints) to the derivation engine.
Audit logging and versioning infrastructure can be implemented using standard enterprise tools or custom development.
open_risks: If worksite mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation engine may produce invalid or unsafe performance targets. Mitigation: Implement validation rules and fallback to conservative defaults.
Centralized derivation engine may become a bottleneck if computation is expensive or updates are frequent. Mitigation: Pre-compute common scenarios; cache results.
High safety criticality demands formal verification of derivation algorithms. Mitigation: Allocate dedicated verification effort; consider third-party validation.
NGHE Solution module scope may be unclear or too coarse-grained. Mitigation: Explicitly define module boundary and responsibilities in system architecture document.
Vehicle and Safety Services modules may resist providing inputs if they perceive loss of autonomy. Mitigation: Establish clear, well-documented input/output contracts.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of worksite mission context input schema (JSON schema, XSD, or equivalent).
Algorithm specifications for cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, and safety margin derivation (mathematical models, pseudocode, validation data).
Configuration database schema and baseline metadata structure (ER diagram, SQL DDL, or equivalent).
Audit log schema and retention policy (log entry format, retention period, compliance requirements).
Interface specifications for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link (data schemas, synchronization protocols, error handling).
Formal verification plan for safety-critical derivation algorithms (test cases, formal methods, third-party validation).
NGHE Solution module architecture document (scope, responsibilities, internal subsystems, ownership).
